initialize(address,address,bytes).details: This function MUST be called immediately after deployment (or via proxy initializer), setting up the policy with the address of the policy engine, initial ownership, and any configuration parameters required by the policy.

params.configParams: Arbitrary encoded configuration parameters to initialize the policy logic.
params.initialOwner: The address that will be assigned as the initial owner of the policy contract.
params.policyEngine: The address of the policy engine that will invoke this policy.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

params.caller: The address of the account which is calling the subject protected by the policy engine.
params.context: Additional information or authorization to perform the operation.
params.parameters: The parameters to use for running the policy.
params.selector: The selector of the method being called on the protected contract.
params.subject: The address of the contract which is being protected by the policy engine.

returns._0: The result of running the policy.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

returns._0: A string representing the type and version of the policy.

onInstall(bytes4).notice: Hook called upon installation of the policy.

onUninstall(bytes4).notice: Hook called upon uninstallation of the policy.

typeAndVersion().notice: Returns the type and version of the policy.
